title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 632.400 - Reexamination of detained person
text:
632.400.  Reexamination of detained person. — Any respondent ordered detained for ninety-day or one-year periods of involuntary inpatient treatment or ordered detained for a period of up to one hundred eighty days of outpatient detention and treatment under this chapter shall be entitled to a reexamination of the order for his or her detention on his or her own motion, or that of his or her legal guardian, parent, spouse, relative, friend or attorney to the court.  Upon receipt of the motion, the court shall conduct or cause to be conducted by a special commissioner proceedings in accordance with section 632.340.
(L. 1980 H.B. 1724, A.L. 1996 S.B. 884 & 841, A.L. 2023 S.B. 106)
